title: Phylogenetic Likelihood Inference
source_data_quality_note: Source MD is a widefield-fluorescence fan-out artifact; principle grounded in domain literature.

E.description: Phylogenetic Likelihood Inference: Phylogenetic maximum likelihood: reconstruct evolutionary tree topology and branch lengths from DNA/protein alignment. The forward operator produces the measurement through a 3-node primitive DAG (M.felsenstein.pruning_algorithm…); recovery is posed as a nonlinear_inverse problem. Difficulty tier delta=5 with effective condition number kappa_eff~2000; model_misspecification_substitution, long_branch_attraction set the accuracy floor at the Omega boundary. See the forward_model field for the closed-form equation.
E.forward_model: L(tree, theta) = prod_site prod_node P(obs_node | obs_children, branch_length, substitution_model)
E.world_state_x: tree topology tau, branch lengths t, substitution model parameters (GTR rates, base frequencies)
E.observation_y: multiple sequence alignment (MSA) with N_taxa taxa and N_sites alignment positions

W.regime: Existence of the recovered phylogenetic_tree_topology is guaranteed within the declared Omega bounds. Uniqueness holds on the measurement-supported subspace; out-of-support modes are controlled by declared priors. Stability is conditionally stable (kappa_eff ~= 2000); model_misspecification_substitution dominates the stability cliff; the remaining mismatch parameters contribute higher-order bias terms. Multinomial sets the irreducible data-fidelity floor.

C.error_bound: Data-fidelity bound: RF distance < 10% topology error for 100 taxa at N_sites > 1000. Noise floor: O(sigma / sqrt(kappa_eff)) with kappa_eff~2000. Mismatch-induced bias + C2 * |model_misspecification_substitution|; iterative solvers achieve q=2 convergence (O(1/k^2)) near the optimum; epsilon_fn captures quality degradation across declared Omega.
C.complexity: O(N_taxa ** 2 * N_sites) per tree evaluation with Felsenstein pruning per iteration
